claims.summary: Delivery and read receipts are transport facts, while held or reply receipts are address evidence.
Direct-contact attention requires bounded TTL, cooldown, focus, stop, and right-to-ignore controls.
One-sided address evidence may support bounded attention but cannot establish mutual address.
claims.disposition: implement distinct attention and mutual-microdose evidence gates in the audit

claims.summary: A correspondence semantic microdose remains live sensory dispatch even when mutual receipts exist.
claims.disposition: require typed live authority, safety checks, and Mike/operator Tier 5 approval
